zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Joan Alice Atheward於1463年出生在英國格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托時，她的父親John Humphrey Marston Atheward27歲，母親Jane Elizabeth Warrene34歲。她和John Edward Stephens VII至少有6個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1520年在英國英格蘭享年57歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Joan Alice Atheward于1463年出生在英国格盧斯特斯爾布里斯托时，她的父亲John Humphrey Marston Atheward27岁，母亲Jane Elizabeth Warrene34岁。她和John Edward Stephens VII至少有6个儿子和2个女儿。她去世于1520年在英国英格兰享年57岁。

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Joan Alice Atheward was born in 1463, in Bristol, Gloucestershire, England, her father, John Humphrey Marston Atheward, was 27 and her mother, Jane Elizabeth Warrene, was 34. She had at least 6 sons and 2 daughters with John Edward Stephens VII. She died in 1520, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 57.

Elle a eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles avec John Edward Stephens VII. Elle est décédée en 1520, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 57 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Joan Alice Atheward(이)가 1463년 영국 그루스스터실르 브리스톨에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John Humphrey Marston Atheward(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Jane Elizabeth Warrene(은)는 34세였다. 그녀는 John Edward Stephens VII(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1520년 영국 잉글랜드에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.
